message:
feat(reactor): upgrade browser reactor from scaffold to wake-driven event drainer

Evolve BrowserReactor from a hardcoded stub (poll() -> 0) to a functional
wake-driven reactor with deterministic event draining:

- Add pending_events queue (Mutex<Vec<Event>>) that accumulates synthetic
  events when wake() is called, snapshotting registered interests into
  ready events.
- Implement bounded poll() draining: each poll() call dequeues up to
  max_events_per_poll events from the pending queue, returning them as
  real reactor events instead of always returning an empty vec.
- Add wake coalescing: when configured, repeated wake() calls before the
  next poll() are coalesced to avoid duplicate event generation.
- Update documentation from "scaffold/stub" language to reflect the actual
  implementation status — the reactor now has working registration
  bookkeeping and wake-driven event delivery, with browser host-source
  wiring (fetch/WebSocket callbacks) remaining for follow-on integration.

This unblocks the browser-core crate's reactor integration tests and makes
BrowserReactor usable for deterministic testing of the browser event model
without requiring actual wasm-bindgen host callbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
